steps.title: Identify the defining claim
steps.content: The passage opens noting Bloom championed Crowley's work, then narrows to his praise for Little, Big in an afterword. The key detail: after explaining Schiller's theory (works are either naive OR sentimental), the text immediately asserts that Little, Big “demonstrates that a work can be both.” This blend is what makes the novel special—and implicitly, what impresses Bloom.

steps.title: Match to the answer
steps.content:
Choice B directly matches the text's purpose: it presents the specific reason why a literary critic (Bloom) is impressed by a particular novel (Little, Big)—the novel achieves a rare blend of two modes Schiller thought were distinct. The other choices either describe a comparison the text doesn't make, reverse the text's argument about categories, or invent authorial inspiration details.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
